title: dryopteris ferruginea
intro: Dryopteris ferruginea, also known as rustyback fern or autumn fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris and is native to Asia and Eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dryopteris ferruginea include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. Dryopteris ferruginea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
